data.body: Plan-review PASS (advisory=7, signed). Triage: the major advisory (raised 3x by A1/G6/T8) — 'the two scale cases route to plan-review-finder.eval.yaml but Pass-1 finder does not emit severity/blast_radius' — is partly right and I'm honoring both readings. G-9 (the A1/T5a scale anchors) is FINDER-side rubric: 'high evidenced scale -> T5a FIRES' and 'inflated/invented scale -> T5a does NOT fire' are both fire/no-fire, testable at the finder layer (no_fire_on_good_cases / recall_on_seeded_defects) — so AC3's two finder cases stand and are correct. FP-3(b) (the one-way blast_radius ratchet) is VERIFIER-side (Pass-2 scores blast_radius); the AC proves it via grep (phrasing present), but to remediate the advisory's valid point I ALSO add a blast_radius one-way discrimination pair to plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml (exercised by the existing discriminates_impact_levels scorer). E2/T8 (AC1 greps undefined phrases): supplying the exact G-9 text from the gap report; AC2's proof is literally grep 'never raises' so that phrase goes verbatim in both verifier prompts + verify.py. G1G2 (SeverityAttrs closure-local, no class docstring): correct — the writable doc surface is the Field(description=...) at verify.py:116, which is what I edit. T5e (phrase drift across 4 files): accepted — criterion calibration lives in prompt strings by rebar design (no config-driven lexicon).
